title: Combinators: >, +, ~, space
description: How to reach descendants, direct children, and siblings.

options: Only <a> that is a direct child of <nav>
questions.explanation: A space is the descendant combinator: any <a> inside <nav> at any depth.

options: Only <li> that is a direct child of <ul>
questions.explanation: > is the child combinator — direct children only.

+ is the next-sibling combinator: only the

immediately after

.

that follows the

.

questions.prompt: Which combinator has the LOWEST specificity impact?
questions.explanation: Combinators don't add specificity — only the selectors on either side count.
Both A and B are direct children of a <ul>. The nested <ul> makes B qualify too.

options: 'article p' matches p at any depth inside article; 'article > p' matches only direct children
'article > p' matches at any depth; 'article p' matches only direct

questions.prompt: Target only <a> that are DIRECT children of <nav>.
